data.title: Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário's (BVMF:PLPL3) Returns Have Hit A Wall
data.description:
If we want to find a potential multi-bagger, often there are underlying trends that can provide clues. Firstly, we'd want to identify a growing return on capital employed (ROCE) and then alongside that, an ever-increasing base of capital employed. This shows us that it's a compounding machine, able to continually reinvest its earnings back into the business and generate higher returns. That's why when we briefly looked at Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário's (BVMF:PLPL3) ROCE trend, we were pretty happy with what we saw.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

Understanding Return On Capital Employed (ROCE)

For those who don't know, ROCE is a measure of a company's yearly pre-tax profit (its return), relative to the capital employed in the business. The formula for this calculation on Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário is:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.16 = R$466m ÷ (R$3.8b - R$894m) (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

Therefore, Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário has an ROCE of 16%. On its own, that's a standard return, however it's much better than the 8.7% generated by the Consumer Durables industry.

Check out our latest analysis for Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário

Above you can see how the current ROCE for Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário compares to its prior returns on capital, but there's only so much you can tell from the past. If you'd like to see what analysts are forecasting going forward, you should check out our free analyst report for Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário .

What The Trend Of ROCE Can Tell Us

While the current returns on capital are decent, they haven't changed much. The company has consistently earned 16% for the last five years, and the capital employed within the business has risen 262% in that time. 16% is a pretty standard return, and it provides some comfort knowing that Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário has consistently earned this amount. Over long periods of time, returns like these might not be too exciting, but with consistency they can pay off in terms of share price returns.

In Conclusion...

The main thing to remember is that Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário has proven its ability to continually reinvest at respectable rates of return. On top of that, the stock has rewarded shareholders with a remarkable 241% return to those who've held over the last five years. So while investors seem to be recognizing these promising trends, we still believe the stock deserves further research.

One more thing to note, we've identified 1 warning sign with Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário and understanding it should be part of your investment process.

data.title: Mueller Industries Pairs Net Cash Focus With Value Added Growth
data.description:
• Mueller Industries (NYSE:MLI) is prioritizing a net cash position and tighter balance sheet management.
  • The company is focusing on value added manufacturing and disciplined growth to limit expansion risks.
  • Recent capital allocation decisions are aimed at preserving flexibility while still investing in the core business.

Mueller Industries, a manufacturer serving plumbing, HVAC, and industrial markets, is leaning into its role as a value added producer rather than a pure commodity play. That focus, together with a net cash position, can matter if demand across construction and industrial end markets becomes more uneven or if input costs move around. For you as an investor, it means the story is less about short term commodity price moves and more about how the company runs its operations and balance sheet.

By stressing cash generation, measured expansion, and selective capital spending, Mueller Industries is signaling that it wants to stay flexible and avoid stretching its balance sheet. For investors who care about stability and downside protection, it can be useful to watch how consistently the company sticks to these capital allocation priorities over time and how they respond if conditions become more volatile.

For you as an investor, the key takeaway is that Mueller is pairing its value added manufacturing focus with tight balance sheet control. The company reported full year sales of US$4,178.55m and net income of US$765.19m, which gives it room to fund growth internally while keeping a net cash position. That matters in a sector that includes players like Aalberts, Leggett & Platt, and Reliance Steel, where capital intensity and raw material exposure can pressure margins. By keeping copper exposure more about fabrication and product complexity than pure metal prices, Mueller is trying to make its earnings profile less dependent on commodity swings and more on execution and customer relationships.

  • ⚠️ Execution risk if disciplined growth limits capacity or product availability versus competitors that spend more aggressively on expansion.

  • 🎁 Strong cash generation supporting a net cash position and share count reduction, which can give the company flexibility when industry conditions become tougher.
  • 🎁 Emphasis on fabrication, product complexity, and channel reach, which can help differentiate Mueller from more commodity exposed peers and support more stable profitability.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth tracking how consistently Mueller sticks to its capital allocation framework, especially if end market demand slows or competitors lean into capacity growth. Watch the balance between maintaining net cash, funding organic projects, and any returns to shareholders, along with how margins hold up relative to peers when copper prices move. You want to see whether management continues to favor measured growth and operational efficiency rather than chasing volume at the expense of returns.

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider AbbVie (ABBV) After Its Strong Multi‑Year Share Price Run?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether AbbVie is still reasonably priced after its strong run, this article walks through what the current share price could be implying about value.
  • With AbbVie closing at US$231.50 and showing returns of 3.6% over 7 days, 4.3% over 30 days and 24.1% over 1 year, many investors are asking whether the recent share price performance already reflects its prospects or if there is still room based on fundamentals.

These themes help frame why the stock has attracted interest over periods such as the last 3 and 5 years, where returns of 70.5% and 166.9% respectively are part of the discussion around risk and reward.
  • Right now AbbVie scores 2 out of 6 on our valuation checks, as shown in our valuation score. We will look at what different valuation approaches say about the stock and then finish with a way to think about valuation that can give you a clearer overall picture.

AbbVie scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: AbbVie Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A DCF model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows, then discounts them back to today’s dollars to arrive at an estimate of what the whole business might be worth right now.

For AbbVie, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about US$19.9b. Analyst inputs and extrapolated estimates suggest free cash flow of roughly US$24.6b in 2026. It then increases through the forecast period to about US$34.5b in 2035, with values beyond the first five years extrapolated by Simply Wall St from earlier projections.

Discounting those projected cash flows back to today gives an estimated intrinsic value of US$372.03 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$231.50, the DCF output implies AbbVie trades at about a 37.8% discount. This indicates that the shares are undervalued on this model.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests AbbVie is undervalued by 37.8%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 53 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for AbbVie.

Approach 2: AbbVie Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand because it links what you pay for each share to the earnings that the business is currently generating. It gives you a quick sense of how many dollars investors are paying for each dollar of earnings.

What counts as a “normal” P/E will usually reflect how the market views a company’s growth outlook and risk profile. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher risk can keep it lower.

AbbVie currently trades on a P/E of 96.82x, compared with the Biotechs industry average of 22.20x and a peer average of 24.55x. Simply Wall St’s “Fair Ratio” for AbbVie is 38.64x. This proprietary metric aims to estimate an appropriate P/E for the company by tying together factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and specific risk characteristics.

Because the Fair Ratio is tailored to AbbVie, it can be more informative than a broad industry or peer comparison, which treats very different companies as if they were identical. In this case, AbbVie’s current P/E of 96.82x sits well above the 38.64x Fair Ratio. This points to the shares looking expensive on this measure.

Result: OVERVALUED

Approach 1: Etsy Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of the cash a business could generate in the future and discounts those amounts back to what they might be worth today. It is a way of translating future cash flows into a single present value figure.

For Etsy, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is reported at about $645.4 million. Analyst projections and subsequent extrapolations by Simply Wall St extend out to 2035, with free cash flow in 2030 projected at $826.1 million, all in $.

When those projected cash flows are discounted back, the DCF model produces an estimated intrinsic value of around $109.54 per share. Compared with the recent share price of about $45.73, this framework suggests Etsy trades at a discount of roughly 58.3%, which screens as undervalued on this measure alone.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Etsy is undervalued by 58.3%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 53 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Etsy.

Approach 2: Etsy Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Etsy, the P/E ratio is a useful way to gauge what you are paying for each dollar of current earnings. Investors usually accept a higher or lower P/E depending on what they expect for future earnings growth and how risky they feel those earnings might be, so there is a wide range of what can count as a normal or fair P/E.

Etsy currently trades on a P/E of 24.8x. That sits above the Multiline Retail industry average of 19.4x and also above the peer group average of 17.6x. This means the market is placing a higher earnings multiple on Etsy than on many of its peers. On Simply Wall St, the Fair Ratio for Etsy is calculated at 21.1x. This Fair Ratio is a proprietary estimate of what Etsy’s P/E could be, given factors such as its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market value and risk characteristics.

Because the Fair Ratio blends these company specific drivers, it can be a more tailored guide than a simple comparison against industry or peer averages alone. With Etsy’s actual P/E of 24.8x sitting above the Fair Ratio of 21.1x, this lens indicates the shares screen as overvalued on an earnings multiple basis.

Result: OVERVALUED

data.title: Atmus Expands Industrial Reach With Koch Filter Deal And New Board Voice
data.description:
• Atmus Filtration Technologies (NYSE:ATMU) completed the acquisition of Koch Filter Corporation, creating a new Industrial Solutions segment.
  • The company also appointed Heath Sharp to its Board of Directors, adding experience in growing international industrial businesses.

Atmus Filtration Technologies, known for filtration products across transportation and industrial applications, is broadening its reach with the Koch Filter acquisition. The move brings a new Industrial Solutions segment into the mix and signals a wider focus across industrial air filtration and related end markets. For you as an investor, it adds another piece to how NYSE:ATMU is positioning its business mix.

The addition of Heath Sharp to the Board of Directors introduces governance experience that is directly tied to scaling global industrial operations. The impact will depend on execution, and this combination of a new business segment and additional board expertise provides more information to consider when assessing Atmus's future footprint and risk profile.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Atmus Filtration Technologies by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Atmus Filtration Technologies.

We've flagged 1 risk for Atmus Filtration Technologies. See which could impact your investment.

This deal drops into an already busy period for Atmus. The company reported fourth quarter 2025 sales of US$446.6 million and net income of US$48 million, took impairment charges of US$8.4 million on long lived assets, issued 2026 guidance for revenue of roughly US$1.95b to US$2.02b, and affirmed a quarterly dividend of US$0.055 per share. Adding Koch Filter on top of that expands Atmus further into industrial air applications, an area that can behave differently to its transportation heavy aftermarket base. The new Industrial Solutions segment gives Atmus another lever for growth and mix, while the board appointment of Heath Sharp brings direct experience in turning a regional industrial business into a global one. For investors, the key question is how quickly Industrial Solutions contributes in a visible way to revenue, margins and cash flow, and whether integration costs or additional write downs appear as the Koch assets are embedded.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Atmus Filtration Technologies Narrative

  • The acquisition supports the narrative that Atmus is using targeted M&A to extend beyond transportation into broader industrial filtration, widening its addressable market and potentially supporting recurring earnings from more end markets.
  • The impairment charges and integration work that often come with acquisitions could challenge assumptions in the narrative about a smooth shift into higher margin areas if restructuring needs are larger or more prolonged.
  • The creation of a dedicated Industrial Solutions segment and the appointment of Heath Sharp add organizational and governance elements that are not fully reflected in the original storyline, especially around how Atmus might build a deal pipeline anchored on Koch Filter.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Atmus Filtration Technologies to help decide what it is worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Integration risk if Koch Filter requires more investment or restructuring than expected, particularly after the recent US$8.4 million impairment on long lived assets.
  • ⚠️ Balance sheet flexibility, given analysts have flagged debt levels as a risk, which can limit room for further deals if cash needs rise.
  • 🎁 Broader industrial exposure alongside transportation, which can smooth revenue across cycles and support recurring demand for filtration products.
  • 🎁 A board that now includes Heath Sharp, whose experience in scaling an industrial manufacturer internationally may strengthen oversight of M&A and operations.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, investors may want to track how Atmus discloses the performance of the new Industrial Solutions segment, including revenue contribution, margins and any one off integration costs tied to Koch Filter. The 2026 guidance range of US$1.945b to US$2.015b in revenue sets a reference point for how much the deal and any follow on M&A actually show up in the numbers. Board level changes also matter, so it can be useful to watch any shifts in capital allocation between acquisitions, share repurchases and dividends, and to monitor how management describes the industrial filtration pipeline on future earnings calls.

data.title: Ingersoll Rand (IR) Margin Compression Tests Bullish Earnings Growth Narratives
data.description:
Ingersoll Rand (IR) has wrapped up FY 2025 with fourth quarter revenue of US$2.1 billion and basic EPS of US$0.68, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of US$7.7 billion and EPS of US$1.46 that reflect the impact of a one off loss on profitability. The company has seen revenue move from US$1.90 billion and EPS of US$0.57 in Q4 2024 to US$2.1 billion and EPS of US$0.68 in Q4 2025, while trailing net margin has compressed from 11.6% to 7.6% as that US$273.4 million one time hit flows through the numbers. With analysts expecting earnings to grow materially faster than revenue over the next few years, these results set up a margins focused story where investors are watching how quickly profitability can rebuild from here.

See our full analysis for Ingersoll Rand.

With the headline results on the table, the next step is to see how these margins, growth expectations and one off items line up against the most widely held narratives about Ingersoll Rand and where those stories might need updating.

See what the community is saying about Ingersoll Rand

Advertisement

EPS swings underline mixed earnings picture

  • Across FY 2025, basic EPS moved from US$0.46 in Q1 to a loss of US$0.29 in Q2, then to US$0.62 in Q3 and US$0.68 in Q4, showing that the one off US$273.4 million loss sat alongside a wide range of quarterly profitability outcomes.
  • What bullish investors are leaning on is the view that, despite this bumpy EPS path and trailing basic EPS of US$1.46, earnings can grow about 24.2% per year while revenue is forecast at about 5.7% per year. This assumes margin rebuilding on top of a revenue base that has gone from US$1,898.6 million in Q4 2024 to US$2,091.2 million in Q4 2025.
    • Bulls point to expansion into underpenetrated regions and an active M&A program that aims to add 400 to 500 basis points of annualized inorganic revenue in 2025 as support for those faster earnings forecasts.
    • The same bullish view expects profit margins to rise from recent levels toward the mid teens over the next few years, which would be a clear shift from the current trailing net margin of 7.6% that still reflects the one off loss.

Bulls argue that the recent EPS volatility is a temporary step on the way to higher profit margins and faster earnings growth, so if you want to see how that case is built out in full, you can read the detailed bull story here 🐂 Ingersoll Rand Bull Case

Margins under pressure while growth is forecast to accelerate

  • Over the last 12 months, trailing revenue is US$7.7b and trailing net income excluding extra items is US$581.4 million, which works out to a 7.6% net margin compared with 11.6% a year earlier. The current margin base is therefore lower even as analysts expect earnings to grow about 24.2% per year.
  • Bears focus on this margin compression, arguing that the push into new regions and reliance on acquisitions, combined with supply chain and China related pressures, could keep profitability under strain even if revenue continues to trend from US$1,861 million in Q3 2024 to levels around US$2,091.2 million most recently.
    • Critics highlight that weaker volumes and declines in China have already affected certain segments, which lines up with the trailing margin slipping to 7.6% despite revenue growth.
    • Bears also flag that integration costs and potential new tariffs or trade frictions could add to expenses, making it harder for margins to move back toward the low double digit levels referenced in forward looking narratives.

Skeptics warn that the current 7.6% margin and recent regional headwinds leave less room for error, so if you want to see how the cautious case lines this up with future earnings assumptions, it is worth reading the bear side in detail 🐻 Ingersoll Rand Bear Case

Rich valuation leans on forecasts, not trailing numbers

  • With the share price at US$98.52, the stock trades on a P/E of 66.3x based on trailing earnings, compared with peer and industry averages around 30x, and a DCF fair value of about US$89.61 that sits below the current price.
  • The balanced narrative around the stock points out that, for this higher P/E to make sense, the forecast gap between roughly 24.2% annual earnings growth and about 5.7% annual revenue growth needs to be backed by a sustained lift in margins from the current 7.6% level, rather than just one off adjustments.
    • Supporters of the consensus view note that recurring, higher margin aftermarket revenue, which has reached 37% of total, is intended to help drive that margin improvement over time.
    • At the same time, the presence of a large US$273.4 million one off loss and prior goodwill and asset impairments shows how acquisition heavy growth can feed through to reported profitability and feeds into the caution embedded in the DCF fair value of US$89.61.

Next Steps

data.title: Robinhood Chain Puts Tokenized Assets And Prediction Markets At Center Stage
data.description:
• Robinhood Markets launched Robinhood Chain, its own Ethereum Layer-2 blockchain, as it pushes further into on-chain trading.
  • The company is pairing this with a bigger move into prediction markets and tokenized assets, including a stake in a derivatives exchange and a planned joint venture with Susquehanna.
  • The goal is to bring real-world assets like stocks and ETFs on chain with 24/7 trading, instant settlement, and self-custody.

Robinhood Markets (NasdaqGS:HOOD) is making this move at a time when its share price is $75.97, with a 16.4% return over the past year. The stock has seen a 7 day return of an 8.3% decline and a 30 day return of a 36.5% decline, and is also down 34.1% year to date, so the market has recently been repricing expectations around the business.

The launch of Robinhood Chain and the push into prediction markets and tokenized assets show the company leaning further into the crossover between its brokerage roots and on chain finance. For you as an investor, the key questions are how quickly these products gain traction, how they affect revenue mix beyond crypto trading, and what that could mean for NasdaqGS:HOOD over time compared with more traditional brokerages.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Robinhood Markets by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Robinhood Markets.

2 things going right for Robinhood Markets that this headline doesn't cover.

For Robinhood, launching Robinhood Chain right after a mixed fourth quarter puts more weight on whether new products can diversify revenue away from crypto trading. Q4 revenue of US$1.28b and net income of US$605m came in with softer growth than many investors were hoping for, and crypto transaction revenue declined 38% year on year, which helps explain the recent share price reset. At the same time, full year 2025 revenue of US$4.47b and net income of US$1.88b show that the broader business is still generating sizeable profit, supported by options, prediction markets and subscriptions.

Robinhood Chain sits at the intersection of those trends. If tokenized stocks, ETFs and prediction markets settle on Robinhood’s own Ethereum Layer 2 rather than third party rails, Robinhood gains more control over fees, margins and product design. That could matter for competitive positioning against players like Coinbase, Interactive Brokers and Charles Schwab, especially as the company pushes for 24/7 trading and instant settlement. The buyback program, with US$909.51m spent to repurchase about 2.5% of shares, also shows management is willing to return capital while funding this push into tokenization and on chain trading.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Robinhood Markets Narrative

  • Robinhood Chain directly supports the narrative that tokenized assets and prediction markets can become new revenue pillars that broaden Robinhood’s product set and help it evolve beyond a single product trading app.
  • The reliance on regulatory progress for tokenization and event contracts also highlights a narrative risk, as stricter rules or slower approvals could limit how quickly Robinhood can scale these offerings.
  • The shift to owning more of the on chain infrastructure via Robinhood Chain is only lightly reflected in the existing narrative and may not fully capture potential changes in unit economics versus relying on external crypto venues.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Robinhood Markets to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Heavy exposure to crypto and on chain activity, at a time when crypto transaction revenue fell 38% in Q4, means results can be sensitive to digital asset trading volumes.
  • ⚠️ Building prediction markets and tokenized assets into core products depends on regulatory approvals, where changes to rules for event contracts or tokenized securities could slow adoption or add compliance costs.
  • 🎁 Robinhood’s record full year revenue of US$4.47b and net income of US$1.88b provide a profit base that can help fund Robinhood Chain, prediction markets and international expansion without relying solely on external capital.
  • 🎁 The company is widening its product set across options, prediction markets, banking and tokenized assets, which may reduce reliance on any single revenue line and deepen customer relationships over time.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you may want to watch how quickly users and developers adopt Robinhood Chain, especially once stock and ETF tokens migrate over, and whether prediction market volumes continue to grow on top of that infrastructure. Tracking the mix of transaction revenue between crypto, options, prediction markets and tokenized assets will show whether this launch actually diversifies the business. It is also worth keeping an eye on any updates from regulators on tokenization and event contracts, plus how competitors like Coinbase and traditional brokers respond to 24/7, on chain trading.
